priorities: Preservation and growth of family wealth with institutional stability
Long‑term wealth transmission to the next generation
Funding and accelerating neuro‑degenerative (Parkinson’s) research to help his daughter
Maintaining a defensive core of large‑cap, research‑intensive pharma/healthcare equities
redLines: Divest from any holding that reduces or abandons Parkinson’s or broader neuro‑degenerative research funding
Investments that prioritize cheap lifestyle profits over R&D in brain‑disease areas
Any shift that weakens the core large‑cap pharma anchor supporting health‑science breakthroughs
preferredSectors: Large‑cap pharmaceuticals / healthcare with strong neuroscience R&D
Biotech firms focused on neuro‑degenerative disease
avoidedSectors: Consumer‑discretionary / lifestyle companies with low R&D intensity
Industries that do not contribute to medical or scientific advancement
High‑volatility speculative ventures lacking clear health‑impact

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: long‑term (10+ years) focused on multigenerational wealth preservation
investmentBehavior.liquidity: moderate – regular cash‑flow needs for family expenses, art sponsorships and foundation grants, otherwise long‑term allocation

lifeEvents.description: Daughter Chloe was studying abroad and doing well; general family stability noted.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: No immediate change, but reinforces focus on long‑term wealth transmission.

lifeEvents.description: In‑depth discussion on long‑term wealth transmission strategies for the children, with a 12% allocation to global healthcare giants.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Strengthened allocation to healthcare/pharma as a generational anchor.

lifeEvents.description: Daughter Chloe diagnosed with rapidly progressing early‑onset Parkinson’s disease; client expressed that his business means nothing if he cannot save her.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Trigger for a strategic pivot toward neuro‑research investments and philanthropic funding.

lifeEvents.description: Family established a Venture Philanthropy foundation dedicated to neuro‑degenerative research; client demanded that core healthcare holdings actively support brain‑disease scientists.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Mandate to align equity holdings with companies funding Parkinson’s research; potential divestments from non‑aligned firms.

lifeEvents.description: CHF 1 million transferred from private portfolio to the family foundation as an immediate grant to a neuro‑genetics lab in Geneva.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Liquidity reallocation to support foundation; reinforces health‑focused investment narrative.

lifeEvents.description: Client met a biotech firm with a breakthrough Parkinson’s asset and reiterated that large‑cap holdings should act as the primary financial support system while his heart remains with neuroscience breakthroughs.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Potential increase in exposure to high‑quality neuro‑biotech; heightened scrutiny of existing holdings for R&D commitment.

personalProfile.interests: automotive engineering, contemporary art sponsorship, neuroscience and medical research
personalProfile.quirks: becomes intensely emotional when discussing daughter's health; values rigorous data and precise execution
personalProfile.rapportTriggers: mention of cutting‑edge brain‑disease research, large‑cap pharma R&D, art patronage projects
personalProfile.kids' education: daughter Chloe studying abroad (pre‑2023) and now undergoing specialized medical treatment

keyQuotes: Our private wealth is now a weapon to save Chloe.
I want our core healthcare and pharma holdings to actively support the entities pushing the boundaries of brain disease research.
If a company we own ever abandons or defunds Parkinson's research to chase cheap lifestyle profits, it would feel like a personal betrayal of my daughter.
We must keep our capital flowing to the companies leading the fight against this disease.
